title: useIntersection
description: Observes element visibility in the viewport, useful for lazy loading, animations, or triggers.
summary: Detects when an element enters or leaves the viewport.

`,(0,n.jsx)(t,{name:"use-intersection",code:`"use client";\r
import { useCallback, useRef, useState } from "react";\r
\r
export function useIntersection<T extends HTMLElement = any>(options?: ConstructorParameters<typeof IntersectionObserver>[1]) {\r
  const [entry, setEntry] = useState<IntersectionObserverEntry | null>(null);\r
\r
  const observer = useRef<IntersectionObserver | null>(null);\r
\r
  const ref = useCallback(\r
    (element: T | null) => {\r
      if (observer.current) {\r
        observer.current.disconnect();\r
        observer.current = null;\r
      }\r
\r
      if (element === null) {\r
        setEntry(null);\r
        return;\r
      }\r
\r
      observer.current = new IntersectionObserver(([_entry]) => {\r
        setEntry(_entry);\r
      }, options);\r
\r
      observer.current.observe(element);\r
    },\r
    [options]\r
  );\r
\r
  return { ref, entry };\r
}`,ext:".ts",repo:"web/hooks/use-intersection.ts"})]})}function d(r={}){let{wrapper:e}=r.components||{};return e?(0,n.jsx)(e,{...r,children:(0,n.jsx)(u,{...r})}):u(r)}function k(r,e){throw new Error("Expected "+(e?"component":"object")+" `"+r+"` to be defined: you likely forgot to import, pass, or provide it.")}return g(_);})();
